title: Project-scoped candidate decisions & feedback data model
description: Introduce the project-owned data the target ownership boundary calls for but the current schema doesn't yet have: per (item, listing) saved/ignored/shortlisted decisions, and feedback tags such as wrong item/accessory/not relevant. Owned transitively via item_id -> project, exactly like listing_matches, which this table sits alongside.
acceptance_criteria: New table (e.g. listing_decisions: id, listing_match_id or (listing_id, item_id), decision ['saved'|'ignored'|'shortlisted'|null], feedback_tag ['wrong_item'|'accessory'|'not_relevant'|null], decided_at, notes) added via the additive _MIGRATIONS pattern
Decisions are unique per (listing, item) pair, consistent with listing_matches' own uniqueness constraint
Ownership is resolved transitively via item_id -> project_id, exactly like listing_matches and alerts_sent — no separate user_id column
Setting/clearing a decision is covered by the same FEATURE-1031 ownership helper and FEATURE-1032 isolation tests as other project-scoped data
The underlying listing/product row referenced by a decision is unaffected by the decision — recording 'wrong item' feedback never mutates or hides the shared listing/product for other users' projects
notes: New feature added 2026-07-08 as a direct result of sanity-checking the ownership boundary against the product decision: 'saved/ignored/shortlisted decisions' and 'feedback such as wrong item/accessory/not relevant' were named as project-owned data the roadmap should model, but no such table exists in the schema today. Flagging here rather than silently building it without confirmation — confirm scope/shape before implementation.
